sections.title: ही — Only, Exactly, Emphasis
sections.explanation:
The particle ही is placed immediately after the word it emphasizes. It narrows the focus to that specific word, conveying meanings like 'only', 'exactly', 'itself', or 'just'.

BASIC MEANINGS:
  वह ही = he himself / only he
  यह ही = this very one (contracts to यही)
  अभी = right now (अब + ही)
  वहीं = right there (वहाँ + ही)

COMMON CONTRACTIONS:
ही often merges with the preceding word:
  यह + ही = यही (this very)
  वह + ही = वही (that very / the same)
  अब + ही = अभी (right now)
  कब + ही = कभी (ever / sometime)
  यहाँ + ही = यहीं (right here)
  वहाँ + ही = वहीं (right there)

EMPHASIS PATTERNS:
ही can follow any word to emphasize it:
  मैं ही जाऊँगा। = I myself will go. / Only I will go.
  आज ही करो। = Do it today itself. / Do it today only.
  एक ही आदमी आया। = Only one man came.
  चाय ही पीता हूँ। = I drink only tea.
sections.tip: ही always comes after the word it emphasizes, never before. The position of ही changes the meaning: 'मैं ही जाऊँगा' (only I will go) vs. 'मैं आज ही जाऊँगा' (I will go today itself).

examples.en: I myself will do this work. / Only I will do this work.
That same boy had come. (वह + ही = वही)
Let's go right now! (अब + ही = अभी)

exercises.prompt: "मैं ही जाऊँगा" का क्या मतलब है? (What does this sentence mean?)
options: I myself will go. / Only I will go.
exercises.explanation: ही after मैं emphasizes the subject, meaning 'I myself' or 'only I'. The particle भी (not ही) would mean 'also'.

exercises.prompt: आज ___ काम ख़त्म करो। (Finish the work today itself.)
exercises.explanation: ही after आज emphasizes 'today' — meaning the work must be done today itself, not later.

exercises.prompt: वह + ही का संक्षिप्त रूप क्या है? (What is the contracted form of वह + ही?)
exercises.explanation: वह + ही contracts to वही, meaning 'that very one' or 'the same'. वहीं is the contraction of वहाँ + ही (right there).

sections.title: भी — Also, Even, Too
sections.explanation:
The particle भी means 'also', 'too', or 'even'. Like ही, it is placed after the word it modifies.

BASIC USAGE:
  मैं भी जाऊँगा। = I will also go. / I too will go.
  वह भी आई। = She also came.
  यह भी अच्छा है। = This is also good.

MEANING 'EVEN':
In certain contexts, भी conveys 'even':
  बच्चे भी यह जानते हैं। = Even children know this.
  उसने खाना भी नहीं खाया। = He didn't even eat food.

COMMON COMBINATIONS:
  कोई भी = anyone at all
  कुछ भी = anything at all
  कहीं भी = anywhere at all
  कभी भी = anytime at all
  जो भी = whoever / whatever
  चाहे ... भी = even if ...

These combinations create indefinite pronouns that are extremely common in everyday Hindi.
sections.tip: ही narrows focus ('only this'), while भी widens it ('this too'). They are opposites in function: ही excludes, भी includes.

examples.hi: बच्चे भी यह समझ सकते हैं।

exercises.prompt: "उसने पानी भी नहीं पिया" का क्या मतलब है?
exercises.explanation: भी with नहीं creates the meaning 'not even'. He didn't even drink water — emphasizing that he consumed nothing at all.

exercises.prompt: कुछ ___ हो सकता है। (Anything can happen.)
exercises.explanation: कुछ + भी = anything at all. भी after question words and indefinites creates universal/emphatic indefinite pronouns.

exercises.prompt: Which pair correctly shows the difference between ही and भी?
options: ही and भी mean the same thing
exercises.explanation: ही narrows focus to 'only' or 'exactly' that word, while भी widens to include 'also' or 'even'. They are functional opposites.

sections.title: तो and न — Emphasis and Softening
sections.explanation:
तो (EMPHASIS / THEN):
तो is a versatile particle with several functions:

1. EMPHASIS on a word — adds stress or contrast:
  मैं तो जाऊँगा। = I (for my part) will go. / As for me, I will go.
  यह तो बहुत अच्छा है! = This is really very good!

2. CONDITIONAL 'THEN':
  अगर तुम चलो, तो मैं भी चलूँगा। = If you go, then I will also go.

3. TOPIC MARKER — introduces a topic for discussion:
  पैसे तो हैं, लेकिन समय नहीं। = Money, well, there is, but time there isn't.

4. MILD CONTRAST:
  वह तो नहीं आया। = He (on the other hand) didn't come.

न (TAG QUESTION / SOFTENER):
न (or ना) at the end of a sentence softens it into a gentle question or request:
  तुम आओगे न? = You will come, won't you?
  यह अच्छा है न? = This is good, isn't it?
  बैठो न। = Do sit down. (gentle request)

न/ना as a softener makes commands and statements less blunt and more polite or intimate. It is extremely common in casual Hindi conversation.
sections.tip: तो after a subject often signals 'as for X' — it is setting up a contrast. न/ना at the end of a sentence is like the English 'right?', 'won't you?', or 'no?' — it invites agreement.

examples.en: I (for my part) am ready. / As for me, I am ready.

examples.hi: अगर बारिश हो, तो मत जाओ।
examples.en: If it rains, then don't go.

exercises.prompt: "मैं तो जाऊँगा" में तो का क्या काम है? (What is the function of तो here?)
options: It means 'then' in a conditional sentence.
It emphasizes the subject — 'As for me, I will go.'
exercises.explanation: तो after मैं emphasizes the subject, creating a sense of 'as for me' or 'I for my part'. It often implies a contrast with what others might do.

exercises.prompt: तुम आओगे ___? (You will come, won't you?)
exercises.hint: Tag question particle at the end
exercises.explanation: न or ना at the end of a sentence turns it into a gentle tag question, like 'won't you?' or 'right?' in English.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence uses तो as a conditional 'then'?
exercises.explanation: 'अगर वह आए, तो बताना' uses तो as 'then' in a conditional: 'If he comes, then tell (me).' The other sentences use तो for emphasis.
